mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and lowers the opportunity cost of holding risk assets, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as rates remain suppressed.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of near-zero FEDFUNDS show forward returns not statistically different from full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals accelerating labor market repair, which triggers upward revisions to corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 gains over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of UNRATE decline greater than 0.3pp fail to produce positive median 20d forward returns, or hit rate for positive direction falls below 55% over a 3-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based consumer and construction demand recovery that jointly forecasts stronger aggregate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 2% AND HOUST 3-month growth > 5% simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint-trigger signal fails to outperform a linear combination of the two individual series' single-factor forecasts (interaction term coefficient not significant at 10% level in walk-forward regression) or produces negative average forward return in more than 40% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery regime, a sustained rebound in industrial production signals restoration of manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is expected to be much weaker or absent in mid-cycle or late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: In post-recession regimes identified by prior GDPC1 contraction, INDPRO month-over-month growth fails to show positive correlation with forward 60d returns (Pearson r < 0.1), or the effect is statistically indistinguishable from the effect observed in non-recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the CPI index above a 0.5% month-over-month pace signals emerging inflationary pressure that raises expected future policy tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Triggered CPI-acceleration instances show non-negative median 20d forward returns, or the hit rate of negative direction predictions is below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payroll employment reflects expanding aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns as the market prices in stronger near-term corporate revenue.
failure_condition: Sign of month-over-month PAYEMS change shows no significant relationship (Rank IC < 0.02) with forward 5d SPX returns over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales growth signals a self-reinforcing expansion in aggregate demand that amplifies corporate earnings momentum beyond either indicator individually, driving stronger S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 1.5%
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a walk-forward regression predicting forward 60d returns, or the joint-trigger sample underperforms the single-factor GDPC1-only benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime combining near-zero policy rates with elevated unemployment, markets interpret persistent monetary accommodation as a floor under risk assets despite weak labor conditions, producing short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns driven by liquidity support rather than fundamentals; this effect should reverse once labor conditions normalize and rate hikes become anticipated.
regime_hint: zero-rate liquidity regime with labor market slack
failure_condition: During periods satisfying both trigger conditions, forward 5d SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the joint FEDFUNDS-UNRATE interaction term loses significance relative to FEDFUNDS alone in a walk-forward regression.
